summary: High‑energy second set with Lost Sailor>Saint and soaring Truckin'
blurb: The show bursts into life with a blistering Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance segue, followed by an expansive Truckin' jam that feeds into a classic drum/space excursion. Jerry’s lead work is on fire, Phil locks in tight, and Brent adds liquid keyboards throughout.
review: Alpine Valley ’81 delivers one of the season’s most electric outings, especially in the second set where the band rides a wave of nonstop momentum. The Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance transition showcases Jerry’s soaring tone and Brent’s “liquid” keyboard weave, while Phil digs deep with heavy, unusual fingering that pushes the jam forward. Truckin' erupts into a hot, extended groove that feeds directly into Billy Kreutzmann’s iconic whistle intro and a thunderous drum/space segment, culminating in a powerful The Other One that rolls straight into an emotionally resonant Stella Blue. Highlights from the first set include a crisp China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider pair and a soaring Bird Song, while the encore Brokedown Palace rounds out the night with warm harmonies despite some vocal slips elsewhere. Recording quality varies – the front half of the matrix capture is crystal‑clear soundboard, and an all‑soundboard source offers excellent fidelity throughout – but both convey the band’s relentless energy and tight interplay.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings praise the set’s high energy, standout Lost Sailor → Saint sequence, and powerful Truckin' jam, while noting only isolated flaws (lyrical flubs, occasional vocal issues). The overall tone is clearly positive with consistent acclaim across multiple sources, justifying a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp early‑show soundboard capture with excellent clarity; despite later audience filler it still presents the full set in the highest overall fidelity among available sources.

key_highlights: Lost Sailor → Saint Of Circumstance sequence shines with soaring guitar and liquid keyboards
Truckin' expands into a classic 1981 drum jam and post‑drum Caution Jam
China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider jam is tight and energetic
The Other One delivers an extended, improvisational peak before flowing into Stella Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring tone and powerful solos on Lost Sailor/​Saint, China Cat jam, and Truckin'; consistently described as ‘on fire’ throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Heavy, unusual fingering adds punch to jams; tight rhythmic support that keeps the band locked in with Jerry.
band_performance.Bob: Energetic vocal delivery lifts the performance; occasional lyrical flubs on Truckin' and The Other One but strong harmonies on Brokedown Palace.
band_performance.Brent: Liquid keyboard texture, hot solos (notably on Big River), and tight interplay on Lost Sailor; birthday jokes add fun atmosphere.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides solid percussive foundation throughout the set, complementing Billy’s drum excursions.
band_performance.Billy: Classic 1981 drum jam highlighted by a whistle intro on Truckin' and an expansive Drum/Space segment leading into the Caution Jam.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
